req.title: Immunization record
req.statement: A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future.

doc.reasoning: The requirement constrains the administered date of an Immunization record to not be in the future. Three partitions exist: (1) past dates — valid, (2) today — boundary/valid, (3) future dates — invalid (422). EP covers the valid past-date partition and the invalid future-date partition. BVA targets today (upper valid boundary) and tomorrow (lower invalid boundary). STC is applicable when a record transitions from submitted to stored. A DT test combining multiple fields with a future date reinforces the rejection rule across conditions.

tests.name: test_immunization_past_date_accepted
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
administered date is a date well in the past (30 days ago)
actions: Build a minimal FHIR Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime = today - 30 days
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-010: immunization stored when date is in the past
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_immunization_past_date_accepted(clinician_session, base_url, make_immunization_payload):
    past_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = make_immunization_payload(occurrence_date=past_date)
    resp = clinician_session.post(f"{base_url}/Immunization", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Immunization"

tests.name: test_immunization_future_date_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
administered date is a date in the future (30 days from now)
actions: Build a minimal FHIR Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime = today + 30 days
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a diagnostic message referencing the date constraint
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_immunization_future_date_rejected(clinician_session, base_url, make_immunization_payload):
    future_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = make_immunization_payload(occurrence_date=future_date)
    resp = clinician_session.post(f"{base_url}/Immunization", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    diagnostics = str(body)
    assert any(kw in diagnostics.lower() for kw in ["date", "future", "occurrence"])

tests.name: test_immunization_today_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
administered date is exactly today (upper boundary of valid range)
actions: Build a minimal FHIR Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime = today (ISO 8601 date string)
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-010: administered date not in the future — today is the valid upper boundary
BVA: boundary value today (max valid date)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date

def test_immunization_today_boundary_accepted(clinician_session, base_url, make_immunization_payload):
    today = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = make_immunization_payload(occurrence_date=today)
    resp = clinician_session.post(f"{base_url}/Immunization", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Immunization"

tests.name: test_immunization_tomorrow_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
administered date is tomorrow (first value outside the valid range)
actions: Build a minimal FHIR Immunization payload with occurrenceDateTime = today + 1 day
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-010: 422 when date > today — tomorrow is the immediate invalid boundary
BVA: boundary value tomorrow (min invalid date)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_immunization_tomorrow_boundary_rejected(clinician_session, base_url, make_immunization_payload):
    tomorrow = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = make_immunization_payload(occurrence_date=tomorrow)
    resp = clinician_session.post(f"{base_url}/Immunization", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body, "Expected a non-empty error body"

tests.name: test_immunization_stored_state_transition
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
No prior immunization record exists for this patient+vaccine combination in the session
actions: POST a valid Immunization with occurrenceDateTime = today - 1 day
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with a resource id
Retrieved occurrenceDateTime matches the posted value
trace: R-HLT-010: immunization stored — verifies state transition from submitted to persisted/completed
STC: resource transitions from non-existent to stored+completed
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_immunization_stored_state_transition(clinician_session, base_url, make_immunization_payload):
    occurrence = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = make_immunization_payload(occurrence_date=occurrence)
    post_resp = clinician_session.post(f"{base_url}/Immunization", json=payload)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, f"POST failed: {post_resp.text}"
    resource_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = clinician_session.get(f"{base_url}/Immunization/{resource_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"GET failed: {get_resp.text}"
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored.get("status") == "completed"
    assert stored.get("occurrenceDateTime", "").startswith(occurrence)

tests.name: test_immunization_future_date_decision_table
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a clinician (role:clinician)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Payload includes all required FHIR Immunization fields with an invalid future date
actions: Build a fully-populated FHIR Immunization payload (patient, vaccine code, status, performer) with occurrenceDateTime = today + 7 days
POST payload to /Immunization with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and OperationOutcome
expected: HTTP status 422 regardless of other fields being valid
trace: R-HLT-010: date constraint takes precedence — 422 even when all other fields are correct
DT: condition combination — all fields valid EXCEPT date; outcome must be rejection
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_immunization_future_date_decision_table(clinician_session, base_url, make_immunization_payload):
    future = (date.today() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    payload = make_immunization_payload(
        occurrence_date=future,
        status="completed",
        include_performer=True,
        include_patient=True
    )
    resp = clinician_session.post(f"{base_url}/Immunization", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" not in body, "No resource id should be present on rejection"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    severities = [i.get("severity") for i in issues]
    assert "error" in severities, f"Expected error severity in issues: {issues}"
